# Implementation Plan: Button Action Completion

**Branch**: `005-fix-button-actions` | **Date**: 2026-06-23 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/002-fix-button-actions/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by `/speckit.plan` and covers Phase 0/Phase 1 design artifacts. Implementation tasks are generated separately by `/speckit.tasks`.

## Summary

This feature completes and corrects visible button/action behavior across the REM system page by page. The technical approach is to preserve the existing PHP monolith, Tabler visual standard, session/CSRF model, and REM business rules while aligning UI controls with their intended behaviors, visible validation, dependent filters, report periods, confirmation flows, and feedback messages.

No database schema change, new framework, new service, or new REM state/catalog is planned. Data-mutating verification must use controlled test records or confirmation-only walkthroughs because the configured environment points at the official database.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: PHP 7.4+ application; PHP 8.2.12 observed locally.

**Primary Dependencies**: Tabler 1.4 visual shell, Bootstrap-compatible Tabler components, Chart.js for dashboard/report visualizations, Composer dependencies including PhpSpreadsheet and TCPDF.

**Storage**: MySQL database `observaciones_rem` through existing PDO-backed models; runtime uploads/generated files remain outside source-controlled content.

**Testing**: PHP syntax checks for touched PHP files, non-mutating page/action walkthroughs, controlled manual tests for mutating workflows, browser console checks for JavaScript errors.

**Target Platform**: Apache/XAMPP-served PHP web application used by registradores and supervisores.

**Project Type**: Existing server-rendered PHP monolith with local JavaScript enhancements and JSON/download endpoints.

**Performance Goals**: User action feedback should appear immediately for local UI state changes; standard list/filter/report actions should complete within normal operator expectations without ambiguous no-op clicks; import preview for a typical operational file should complete within 30 seconds.

**Constraints**: Preserve Tabler-only UI standard, do not extend deprecated visual CSS, preserve existing roles/permissions/REM meanings, keep CSRF on mutating browser actions, avoid destructive production-data tests unless explicitly approved.

**Scale/Scope**: Ten primary screens: dashboard, observaciones, supervision, reportes, usuarios, asignaciones, eliminadas, establecimientos, perfil, and versionado; all visible buttons, links, dropdown action items, switches, file pickers, and submit controls on those screens.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

- **Source of Truth**: PASS. Plan references `README.md`, `specs/002-fix-button-actions/spec.md`, prior module specs under `specs/`, and current discovered code behavior. Documentation artifacts are updated in this feature directory.
- **RBAC & REM Data Integrity**: PASS. Affected roles are registrador and supervisor. Plan preserves backend authorization, year/month context, assignment scoping, supervision state transitions, soft-delete/permanent-delete distinctions, and report filters.
- **Security & Session Safety**: PASS. Plan explicitly covers session validation, CSRF on mutating actions, upload restrictions, password policy visibility, administrative confirmations, and export/report behavior. No new secrets or config values are introduced.
- **Testable & Reversible Data Changes**: PASS. No schema migration is planned. Mutating checks are required only with controlled test records or confirmation-only walkthroughs. Irreversible actions such as permanent delete and rollback must not be executed against official data without explicit approval.
- **Simple Monolith & Observability**: PASS. Work stays within existing views, shared JavaScript, APIs, models, and documentation. User-visible errors, success messages, audit/history/status behavior, and report queue feedback remain part of the plan.

Post-design re-check: PASS. `research.md`, `data-model.md`, `contracts/action-behavior.md`, and `quickstart.md` keep the same constraints and introduce no constitutional violations.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/002-fix-button-actions/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   └── action-behavior.md
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md
└── spec.md
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
api/             # Existing JSON/download endpoints for auth, observations, reports, users, assignments, deleted records, locations, versioning
models/          # Existing PDO-backed REM domain/data logic
views/           # Existing server-rendered screens with page-specific action scripts
includes/        # Shared layout, CSRF, navigation, breadcrumbs
config/          # Existing constants, configuration, SQL files
assets/js/       # Shared client helpers, charts, theme, notifications
assets/css/      # Tokens, Tabler overrides, legacy stylesheet retained as compatibility only
specs/           # Feature/module specifications and this plan
uploads/         # Runtime files; generated content must not be committed
```

**Structure Decision**: Keep the existing PHP monolith. This feature should make localized corrections in the existing page modules and shared helpers only where behavior is genuinely shared. No new frontend framework, service, or architectural layer is justified.
